title: Common Framework Antipatterns
concept: Over-engineering simple loops, ignoring state serialization, and relying on implicit magic handlers.

exercises.prompt: What is important when choosing a framework for your AI agent?
exercises.explain: Choosing a framework that matches task complexity and scaling needs helps create efficient AI agents.

cards.title: What Are Antipatterns?
cards.body: Antipatterns are common mistakes that can make your work harder or less efficient.

cards.title: Common Antipatterns
cards.body: These are the typical pitfalls in framework usage that can lead to poor performance or extra complexity.

cards.title: Building a House
cards.body: Imagine over-engineering a simple house with complicated blueprints. It makes construction harder than necessary, just like over-complicating loops in your code.

cards.title: Loop Over-Engineering Example
cards.body: Instead of using a simple loop for a basic task, someone adds unnecessary layers of functions.

cards.title: Myth: More Code Equals Better Performance
cards.body: Many believe that more code always means better performance, but that's often not true. Simple solutions can be more effective.

cards.title: State Serialization Issue
cards.body: When saving agent decisions, if you ignore state serialization, you'll face problems later.

left.text: Ignoring state serialization leads to data loss.

right.text: Serialized state keeps data safe and retrievable.

exercises.prompt: What is a common error related to state management in frameworks?

options.text: Resolving all tasks with magic handlers

exercises.explain: Ignoring state serialization can lead to losing important data about your agent's decisions.

exercises.prompt: Over-engineering can make simple loops easier to read.
exercises.explain: Over-engineering actually complicates simple loops and can make the code harder to understand.

exercises.prompt: It's crucial to avoid __________ in your code to keep it efficient.
exercises.sentence: It's crucial to avoid over-engineering in your code to keep it efficient.
exercises.explain: Choosing to avoid over-engineering helps maintain code clarity and efficiency.

exercises.prompt: Which option best represents a common antipattern?

options.text: Using magic handlers for every task

exercises.explain: Using magic handlers without understanding can lead to unexpected behavior in your agents.

exercises.prompt: What is a consequence of relying on implicit magic handlers?

options.text: They make code easier to debug

exercises.explain: Relying on implicit magic handlers can cause unexpected behavior in your AI agents, making it hard to predict how they will act.
